commit_message:
[ML] Replace metric with count (#146185)

The detector is now using count rather than metric. The test only exists
to verify internal indices are created, so it doesn't matter which
detector is used. count is quicker because it just sums events per
bucket, rather than metric which has a greater chance of failure while
it is calculating min, max, mean, etc.

Resolve #133320

issue_title: Update to a running transform's frequency does not take effect until it is stopped and restarted
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.18.3

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

Chainguard 5.15.0-1086-azure (Elastic Cloud)

### Problem Description

It is possible to update a running transform by posting to its `_update` API. 

[Docs](https://www.elastic.co/docs/api/doc/elasticsearch/operation/operation-transform-update-transform) say:
> All updated properties except description do not take effect until after the transform starts the next checkpoint, thus there is data consistency in each checkpoint.

However - we have found that updating the `frequency` parameter does not take effect until the transform is stopped and restarted. 

### Steps to Reproduce

Enable trace logging for `TransformTask`:

```
PUT _cluster/settings
{
  "transient": {
    "logger.org.elasticsearch.xpack.transform.transforms.TransformTask": "TRACE"
  }
}
```

- Define a transform with `frequency: 1m`
- Start the transform
- Observe trigger pattern in logs (some log message with format `"[%s] triggered(event=%s) "`)
- Post an update to the transform to reduce frequency to 1 second. 
- Note no change to triggering behaviour in logs.

### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_
commit_message:
[Transform] Allow dynamic updates to frequency (#136757)

Updates to a continuous transform's frequency should go into effect for
the next transform's checkpoint without needed to stop and restart the
transform.

Fix #133321

commit_message:
Fix transport version tests on windows (#134739)

Running on windows has some slightly different behavior when interacting
with the filesystem. In particular, this commit fixes the changed
resources prefix when looking at changed resources in git to always use
forward slashes as git outputs them. It also fixes the case that CI uses
a non-main (eg master) branch as the default branch. Finally it fixes
git diff so it outputs paths relative to the transport resources dir, as
ls-files does.

commit_message:
[ML] Fix test InferenceIT#testInferClassificationModel (#144919)

InferenceIT.cleanUpData() calls resetFeatures() without the subsequent waitForPendingTasks() call that every other test in the same module includes. This allows the next test method to start while the cluster is still processing the feature reset, causing transient node_not_connected_exception when putModel() tries to retrieve task information across all nodes.

commit_message:
Ensure enough nodes in `WriteLoadForeacasterIT` (#134132)

These tests would sometimes create 1-replica indices in a 1-node cluster
which will never reach `green` health. This commit ensures there are
enough nodes to allocate all the shards.

Closes #133455 Closes #134124 Closes #134123

issue_body:
### Description

A simple query like the following will result in an error:

```
FROM books
| EVAL s = score(match(title, "Tolkien"))
```

error:
```
[2025-08-22T17:48:16,126][ERROR][o.e.b.ElasticsearchUncaughtExceptionHandler] [runTask-0] fatal error in thread [elasticsearch[runTask-0][search_coordination][T#1]], exiting java.lang.AssertionError: LuceneQueryEvaluator requires shard information
        at org.elasticsearch.compute.lucene.LuceneQueryEvaluator.<init>(LuceneQueryEvaluator.java:57)
        at org.elasticsearch.compute.lucene.LuceneQueryScoreEvaluator.<init>(LuceneQueryScoreEvaluator.java:35)
        at org.elasticsearch.compute.lucene.LuceneQueryScoreEvaluator$Factory.get(LuceneQueryScoreEvaluator.java:71)
```

This most likely has to do with how Evals are pushed down, such that we then try to execute `score` without the shard information.

`extract_snippet` will most likely have the same error - so we should find a generic solution that can be applied to any function that requires shard info in order to be evaluated.

The same needs to be done for values which are provide as null_values, so that the null_value replacement and regular values can be tested correctly for equality.

commit_message:
Extract `AbstractGeoIpDownloader` for shared concurrency logic (#137660)

In #134223, we modified the concurrency design of the `EnterpriseGeoIpDownloader`. Before that, both `EnterpriseGeoIpDownloader` and `GeoIpDownloader` shared the exact same concurrency implementation. In this PR, we move the improved concurrency design of `EnterpriseGeoIpDownloader` to `AbstractGeoIpDownloader` and make `GeoIpDownloader` extend this class as well. This way, both classes are aligned on the concurrency implementation and will both benefit from any future improvements/modifications.

There is likely more logic in these two classes that can be moved to the abstract class, but I'm leaving that for future work, to keep the scope of this PR limited to the concurrency improvements.

Incidentally, I am optimistic that this will resolve the following test failures. I've been running the GeoIP YAML test suites for thousands of iterations on `main`, but haven't been able to reproduce the failures at all. The reported failure messages are almost exactly the same as the one in #126124, which was addressed by #134223. Therefore, I'm closing these issues for now.
Closes #130681
Closes #133597
Closes #135132
Closes #135158
